title: ThesisStudio Lay-Translator Manifest
description: Audit trail written next to a lay-translator output (08_OUTPUT/layman/*.manifest.json). Records the source-article provenance, the citation-library version used during translation, and the translator's editorial decisions, so a downstream consumer (BlogStudio or any blog-style importer) can verify the lay version against its source.

translated_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime when the translation was produced.

commit.description: Git SHA of the source article at translation time. 'uncommitted' if working tree had uncommitted changes.

git_dirty.description: True if the working tree had uncommitted changes at translation time.

body_files.description: Repo-relative paths to the source article body files used as input.

rendered_source.description: Repo-relative path to a rendered markdown source if used (e.g., 08_OUTPUT/markdown/thesis.md), else null when the translator worked from 03_BODY/ directly.

references_bib.description: Repo-relative path to the BibTeX library used for resolving citations into the lay version's Sources section.

references_bib_hash.description: Hash of the BibTeX library at translation time (md5, sha1, or sha256).

section_grouping_map.description: Maps each lay-version section heading to one or more source-article section identifiers (e.g., '§1', '§3.1'). Lets a downstream consumer link 'see the academic source' from any lay section back to its origin.

verbatim_phrases_preserved.description: Exact strings carried through verbatim from source to lay version (hinge sentences, lead-author posture quotes, structural-finding sentences). Empty array is valid (no verbatim required).

translator_flags.description: Short list of judgment-call flags surfaced for lead-author editorial review (passages where compression risked precision loss, unresolved verbatim attributions, etc.). Empty array is valid (clean translation, no flags raised).

teaser_path.description: Repo-relative path to the teaser variant if produced via --teaser mode, else null. Reserved for the future --teaser feature.
